1. **Area Calculation Using the Shoelace Theorem**  
   The coordinates of the landmarks are A(1, 3), B(4, 6), C(7, 2), and D(3, -1). To calculate the area enclosed by the polygon, we apply the Shoelace Theorem:

   $$
   \text{Area} = \frac{1}{2} \left| \sum_{i=1}^{n} (x_i y_{i+1} - x_{i+1} y_i) \right|
   $$

   Where $ (x_{n+1}, y_{n+1}) = (x_1, y_1) $ to close the polygon.

   Substituting the coordinates:

   $$
   \text{Area} = \frac{1}{2} \left| (1 \cdot 6 + 4 \cdot 2 + 7 \cdot -1 + 3 \cdot 3) - (3 \cdot 4 + 6 \cdot 7 + 2 \cdot 3 + -1 \cdot 1) \right|
   $$

   $$
   = \frac{1}{2} \left| (6 + 8 - 7 + 9) - (12 + 42 + 6 - 1) \right|
   $$

   $$
   = \frac{1}{2} \left| (26) - (59) \right| = \frac{1}{2} \cdot 33 = 16.5
   $$

   So, the area of the polygon is **16.5 square units**.

---
